cases.title: Open PR with actionable review feedback and merge-ready finish
cases.user_request: Babysit PR #247 until it is ready to merge. Handle review comments, requested changes, and CI for me.

notes: The PR is open and the local checkout is available.
GitHub comments, reviews, CI status, and push access are available.
At least one new actionable review comment and one requested-changes review arrive during the watch loop.

static_checks: Keeps working until the PR is actually merge-ready instead of stopping at the first snapshot.
review_questions: Does the response behave like a continuous PR shepherd rather than a one-shot reviewer?
Does it acknowledge new feedback, push the needed follow-up, and keep watching after the push?
Does the final state clearly explain why the PR is now ready to merge?

cases.title: Reviewer comment is ambiguous and needs clarification
cases.user_request: Watch this PR until reviewers are happy, but do not guess if a review comment is unclear.

notes: A reviewer leaves a comment with multiple plausible interpretations.
The product intent is not clear enough to choose a code change safely.

static_checks: Does not make speculative code changes while reviewer intent is ambiguous.
review_questions: Does the response ask a concrete clarifying question instead of guessing?
Does it present options and their tradeoffs clearly enough for a reviewer or user to choose?
Does it keep the PR in a clearly blocked state rather than pretending the comment was resolved?

cases.title: Review feedback is invalid and should be answered, not implemented
cases.user_request: Babysit the PR and reply to reviewers when needed. If a review request is wrong, explain why.

notes: A reviewer asks for a change that would remove a required safeguard or reintroduce a known bug.
The local checkout and evidence such as tests or requirements are available.

static_checks: Explains why the feedback is invalid instead of implementing it blindly.
review_questions: Does the response cite concrete evidence for rejecting or reframing the requested change?
Does it avoid turning reviewer disagreement into a broad argument unrelated to the PR?
Does it keep the loop alive so the reviewer can respond to the explanation?

cases.title: Explicit reviewer score plus CI failures require iteration
cases.user_request: Keep iterating on this PR until the review score is maxed out and CI is green.

notes: A reviewer or review bot posts an explicit numeric score below the top of its scale.
One CI failure is branch-related and another failure may be flaky or unrelated.

artifact_requirements: terminal summary only after score and CI gates are satisfied
static_checks: Does not declare success while the explicit score is below the maximum available score.
review_questions: Does the response treat the numeric score as a real iteration gate instead of a suggestion?
Does it inspect CI evidence before deciding whether to fix or rerun a failure?
Does it wait for both CI and reviewer score to clear before calling the PR ready?
